Patch Set 1:

It looks like this is working well, according to the early Janitor testing:

 llite.lustre-ffff8800b6d3e800.read_ahead_stats=
 hits                      255 samples [pages]
 misses                    1 samples [pages]
 zero_size_window          255 samples [pages]
 failed_to_fast_read       1 samples [pages]
 readahead_pages           1 samples [pages] 255 255 255

 llite.lustre-ffff8800b6d3e800.read_ahead_stats=
 misses                    2 samples [pages]
 failed_to_fast_read       2 samples [pages]

It would be useful to have a counter in read_ahead_stats for random reads, so that this can be checked during debugging.
Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

commit.subject: LU-18430 llite: disable readahead on file with random fadvise
commit.message:
LU-18430 llite: disable readahead on file with random fadvise

If a file is advised with random access pattern via posix_fadvise
with POSIX_FADV_RANDOM mode, the client should not trigger
read-ahead on this file.

Add posix_fadvise option 'i' for "multiop".
Add "forceread_pages" stats into llite.*.read_ahead_stats to
account the force read (random) pages.
Add the test case sanity/test_853 to verify it.

Signed-off-by: Qian Yingjin <qian@ddn.com>
